steps.title: Understand what we're testing
steps.content: To find the correct table, all three $(x, y)$ pairs must satisfy the equation $y = -4x + 40$. We can test a choice by substituting each $x$ value into the equation and checking if we get the matching $y$ value.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice C
steps.content:
Substitute each point into $y = -4x + 40$:
  • $x = 0$: $y = -4(0) + 40 = 40$ ✓ (table shows \$40$)
  • $x = 1$: $y = -4(1) + 40 = 36$ ✓ (table shows \$36$)
  • $x = 2$: $y = -4(2) + 40 = 32$ ✓ (table shows \$32$)
All three points satisfy the equation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
